model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Michigan is the narrow pick because the Wolverines own a tiny but meaningful season-long edge in the predictive metrics, ranking just ahead of Arizona in both KenPom and Torvik while pairing an elite offense with the slightly better defensive baseline. Arizona absolutely has a credible path if its pace and shot-making push this into a higher-variance game, but the lingering Koa Peat injury concern adds a bit more uncertainty on the Wildcats’ side than on Michigan’s. In a matchup this close, I’d lean to Michigan’s marginally stronger two-way profile.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan over Arizona in a near toss-up
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan gets the lean in an extremely close semifinal because its full-season efficiency profile is slightly stronger and its health outlook appears a bit cleaner.
evidence: Michigan is No. 2 in both KenPom and Torvik, while Arizona is No. 3 in both systems.
Michigan has the slightly better overall efficiency margin and defensive baseline, with KenPom adjD 88.4 versus Arizona’s 89.7.
Arizona’s offense is strong enough to create a real upset path by speeding the game up and winning the shot-making battle.
Recent reporting flagged Arizona star Koa Peat with a lower-body injury concern, while Michigan’s Yaxel Lendeborg was reported expected to play through his issue.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.title: Arizona No. 1 seed faces major test after Koa Peat injury
result.snippet: A recent home win over BYU pushed Arizona to 24-2 and completed a season sweep of the Cougars, but the Wildcats now face a stretch of opponents that will test depth, endurance and adaptability. A nagging lower-leg injury to freshman forward Koa Peat complicates matters, forcing Arizona to adjust its rotation at a critical moment.

result.title: Arizona Wildcats Lose Koa Peat to Leg Injury - Tucson Today
result.snippet: The University of Arizona announced that freshman forward Koa Peat will miss at least the next two games due to a leg muscle strain. Peat's injury was first noticed during the team's overtime loss to Texas Tech on Saturday, where he played limited minutes. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: f4-2
Current game label: National semifinal 2
Round: Final Four
Resolved teams: Arizona (arizona) vs Michigan (michigan)
Prior committed picks: 65
